# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!
  
  ALLOWED_TABLES = %w[users orders products transactions invoices].freeze
  ALLOWED_COLUMNS = {
    'users' => %w[id email created_at updated_at status role],
    'orders' => %w[id user_id total status created_at updated_at],
    'products' => %w[id name price category created_at],
    'transactions' => %w[id order_id amount status created_at],
    'invoices' => %w[id order_id total paid_at created_at]
  }.freeze
  ALLOWED_OPERATORS = %w[= != > < >= <= LIKE IN NOT\ IN IS\ NULL IS\ NOT\ NULL].freeze
  ALLOWED_AGGREGATES = %w[COUNT SUM AVG MIN MAX].freeze
  ALLOWED_ORDER_DIRECTIONS = %w[ASC DESC].freeze
  
  def create
    query_params = parse_query_params(params[:query])
    
    unless query_params[:valid]
      render json: { error: query_params[:error] }, status: :bad_request
      return
    end
    
    sql = build_safe_query(query_params)
    
    begin
      results = execute_safe_query(sql, query_params[:bind_values])
      render json: { data: results, query: sql }
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      render json: { error: 'Query execution failed' }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end
  
  private
  
  def authenticate_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render json: { error: 'Unauthorized' }, status: :unauthorized
    end
  end
  
  def parse_query_params(query_json)
    query_data = JSON.parse(query_json)
    
    table = query_data['table']
    unless ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(table)
      return { valid: false, error: 'Invalid table name' }
    end
    
    select_columns = query_data['select'] || []
    validated_columns = validate_columns(table, select_columns)
    unless validated_columns[:valid]
      return validated_columns
    end
    
    where_conditions = query_data['where'] || []
    validated_where = validate_where_conditions(table, where_conditions)
    unless validated_where[:valid]
      return validated_where
    end
    
    group_by = query_data['group_by'] || []
    validated_group = validate_columns(table, group_by)
    unless validated_group[:valid]
      return { valid: false, error: 'Invalid group by columns' }
    end
    
    order_by = query_data['order_by'] || []
    validated_order = validate_order_by(table, order_by)
    unless validated_order[:valid]
      return validated_order
    end
    
    limit = query_data['limit']&.to_i || 1000
    limit = [limit, 10000].min
    
    {
      valid: true,
      table: table,
      select: validated_columns[:columns],
      where: validated_where[:conditions],
      bind_values: validated_where[:bind_values],
      group_by: validated_group[:columns],
      order_by: validated_order[:order_clauses],
      limit: limit
    }
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    { valid: false, error: 'Invalid JSON format' }
  end
  
  def validate_columns(table, columns)
    return { valid: true, columns: ['*'] } if columns.empty? || columns.include?('*')
    
    validated = []
    columns.each do |col|
      if col.is_a?(Hash) && col['aggregate']
        aggregate = col['aggregate'].upcase
        column = col['column']
        alias_name = col['alias']
        
        unless ALLOWED_AGGREGATES.include?(aggregate)
          return { valid: false, error: "Invalid aggregate function: #{aggregate}" }
        end
        
        unless column == '*' || ALLOWED_COLUMNS[table]&.include?(column)
          return { valid: false, error: "Invalid column for aggregate: #{column}" }
        end
        
        if alias_name && !alias_name.match?(/\A[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*\z/i)
          return { valid: false, error: "Invalid alias name: #{alias_name}" }
        end
        
        validated << { aggregate: aggregate, column: column, alias: alias_name }
      else
        unless ALLOWED_COLUMNS[table]&.include?(col)
          return { valid: false, error: "Invalid column: #{col}" }
        end
        validated << col
      end
    end
    
    { valid: true, columns: validated }
  end
  
  def validate_where_conditions(table, conditions)
    bind_values = []
    validated_conditions = []
    
    conditions.each do |condition|
      column = condition['column']
      operator = condition['operator']&.upcase
      value = condition['value']
      
      unless ALLOWED_COLUMNS[table]&.include?(column)
        return { valid: false, error: "Invalid where column: #{column}" }
      end
      
      unless ALLOWED_OPERATORS.include?(operator)
        return { valid: false, error: "Invalid operator: #{operator}" }
      end
      
      if operator == 'IN' || operator == 'NOT IN'
        unless value.is_a?(Array)
          return { valid: false, error: "#{operator} requires array value" }
        end
        validated_conditions << { column: column, operator: operator, placeholder_count: value.length }
        bind_values.concat(value)
      elsif operator == 'IS NULL' || operator == 'IS NOT NULL'
        validated_conditions << { column: column, operator: operator, placeholder_count: 0 }
      else
        validated_conditions << { column: column, operator: operator, placeholder_count: 1 }
        bind_values << value
      end
    end
    
    { valid: true, conditions: validated_conditions, bind_values: bind_values }
  end
  
  def validate_order_by(table, order_clauses)
    validated = []
    
    order_clauses.each do |clause|
      column = clause['column']
      direction = clause['direction']&.upcase || 'ASC'
      
      unless ALLOWED_COLUMNS[table]&.include?(column)
        return { valid: false, error: "Invalid order by column: #{column}" }
      end
      
      unless ALLOWED_ORDER_DIRECTIONS.include?(direction)
        return { valid: false, error: "Invalid order direction: #{direction}" }
      end
      
      validated << { column: column, direction: direction }
    end
    
    { valid: true, order_clauses: validated }
  end
  
  def build_safe_query(params)
    sql_parts = []
    
    select_clause = build_select_clause(params[:select])
    sql_parts << "SELECT #{select_clause}"
    sql_parts << "FROM #{params[:table]}"
    
    if params[:where].any?
      where_clause = build_where_clause(params[:where])
      sql_parts << "WHERE #{where_clause}"
    end
    
    if params[:group_by].any?
      group_clause = params[:group_by].map { |col| col.is_a?(String) ? col : col[:column] }.join(', ')
      sql_parts << "GROUP BY #{group_clause}"
    end
    
    if params[:order_by].any?
      order_clause = params[:order_by].map { |o| "#{o[:column]} #{o[:direction]}" }.join(', ')
      sql_parts << "ORDER BY #{order_clause}"
    end
    
    sql_parts << "LIMIT #{params[:limit]}"
    
    sql_parts.join(' ')
  end
  
  def build_select_clause(columns)
    return '*' if columns == ['*']
    
    columns.map do |col|
      if col.is_a?(Hash)
        aggregate_expr = "#{col[:aggregate]}(#{col[:column]})"
        col[:alias] ? "#{aggregate_expr} AS #{col[:alias]}" : aggregate_expr
      else
        col
      end
    end.join(', ')
  end
  
  def build_where_clause(conditions)
    conditions.map do |condition|
      if condition[:operator] == 'IN'
        placeholders = (['?'] * condition[:placeholder_count]).join(', ')
        "#{condition[:column]} IN (#{placeholders})"
      elsif condition[:operator] == 'NOT IN'
        placeholders = (['?'] * condition[:placeholder_count]).join(', ')
        "#{condition[:column]} NOT IN (#{placeholders})"
      elsif condition[:operator] == 'IS NULL' || condition[:operator] == 'IS NOT NULL'
        "#{condition[:column]} #{condition[:operator]}"
      else
        "#{condition[:column]} #{condition[:operator]} ?"
      end
    end.join(' AND ')
  end
  
  def execute_safe_query(sql, bind_values)
    sanitized_sql = ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_array([sql] + bind_values)
    result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(sanitized_sql)
    result.to_a
  end
end
